meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea. Learn how to engineer SaaS pricing models to validate demand and monetize your MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, pick a niche with high-intent spenders and deploy a tiered pricing model immediately. By integrating Stripe via Antigravity and architecting with Claude Code, you validate market demand through revenue rather than speculation. Build, price, and pivot based on real-world transaction data.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’re waiting for a spark, but the reality of the SaaS landscape is that ideas are cheap; execution and validation are the only currencies that matter. Stop hunting for a unicorn and start building a business that solves a painful, profitable problem.

True founders don't wait for inspiration—they engineer it. By shifting your focus from 'what should I build' to 'who will pay for what solution,' you unlock a systematic approach to product-market fit. We’ll use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate your stack, turning your pricing model into your primary tool for market discovery.

steps.title: Define High-Intent Niche Parameters
steps.description: Identify a vertical where manual processes are costing companies thousands. Focus on B2B workflows where ROI is measurable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a list of 5 high-churn B2B niches with low technical saturation and high willingness-to-pay for automation tools.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Analyze the competitive landscape for automated invoice reconciliation tools for small architectural firms.'

steps.title: Architect the Tiered Pricing Model
steps.description: Design a three-tier pricing structure (Starter, Pro, Scale) that anchors value on usage metrics rather than feature lists.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe-compatible pricing schema for a SaaS tool using usage-based billing tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file pricing_config.ts --instruction 'Implement a tiered subscription logic with Stripe Checkout integration.'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Core
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate, focusing on auth and the primary value-add feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js application with Supabase auth and a clean dashboard UI for a SaaS MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure and set up database migrations for user subscriptions.'

steps.title: Implement Conversion-Driven UI
steps.description: Ensure your landing page explicitly targets the 'pain' identified in step 1, using your pricing as a conversion anchor.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a high-converting landing page section that highlights the ROI of our SaaS product.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file landing.tsx --instruction 'Add a pricing table component with a clear CTA for the Pro tier.'

steps.title: Automate Feedback Loops
steps.description: Connect user signups to a CRM or Slack channel to capture qualitative feedback immediately upon payment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a webhook handler for Stripe events to notify Slack when a new user hits a specific usage tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a webhook endpoint that logs Stripe subscription events to the database.'

pro_tips: Always price your MVP higher than you think; low pricing attracts low-quality customers who demand the most support.
Use 'Price Anchoring' by placing your most profitable plan in the center and highlighting it as the 'Most Popular' choice.
If you have no project ideas, build a tool that automates one task you currently perform for your own business.
Track 'Time-to-First-Dollar' as your primary KPI; if it takes more than 14 days to launch, your scope is too large.
Offer a 'Founder’s Lifetime Deal' to your first 10 users to generate immediate cash flow and deep, honest feedback.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to Vercel, set up your Stripe dashboard, and reach out to 50 potential users in your identified niche via cold email or direct outreach.
content.conversion_block: Stop overthinking the idea and start engineering the revenue. The market is waiting for someone to solve their problem—make sure it's you. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
